visible_evidence: Page 1 presents the title, anonymous ACL header, abstract, and opening introduction without visible clipping, overlap, or detached text.
The title and first abstract sentences foreground vision-language grounding, embodied-data reliability, and controlled material perturbation rather than a standalone asset-conversion tool.
Page 2 keeps Figure 1 readable at page scale, including the accepted low-text v18 schematic and the exact Target: box label.
The contribution list now flows naturally after Figure 1 without a manual Introduction page break.
The four-gate paragraph and contribution list remain readable and keep the claim boundary explicit.
main_risks: The contribution remains a bounded case-study protocol, so final human review should judge whether the ACL-family framing is persuasive enough.
Final route and OpenReview author fields remain human-only blockers.
imagegen_decision: No new imagegen iteration was run because the accepted v18 Figure 1 still passes this first-page visual-fit check.

claim_boundary.may_support: first-page ACL fit for a claim-bounded VLM grounding and embodied-data reliability paper
